flashcards.front: What are the three levels of the three-schema architecture?
flashcards.back: External (user views), Conceptual (logical structure), and Internal (physical storage) levels.
Ability to modify conceptual schema without affecting external schemas or application programs.

flashcards.front: Which schema level describes the physical storage details?
What does the conceptual schema define?
flashcards.back: The logical structure of the entire database for all users.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of mappings in DBMS architecture?
flashcards.back: To link different schema levels and enable data independence.

flashcards.front: Name two types of data independence.
What does ANSI/SPARC architecture refer to?
flashcards.back: The standardized three-level database architecture framework.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between schema and instance?
flashcards.back: Schema is the structure, instance is the actual data at a given time.

flashcards.front: Which data model uses a tree structure?
What is a key advantage of the relational model?
flashcards.back: Simple tabular structure with easy data retrieval using SQL.

flashcards.front: What does the Entity-Relationship model represent?
flashcards.back: Entities, their attributes, and relationships between them.
Ability to modify internal schema without affecting conceptual schema.
